skill.name: case-control-study-quality-assessment-nos
skill.description: Clinical Research Bias Assessment - Case-Control Study (NOS) v2.3.0. Use when you need to assess the bias of a case-control study using the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale (NOS) criteria, or when evaluating the quality of a medical paper.
skill.summary: Evaluates case-control study quality using the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale (NOS) across three domains: selection, comparability, and exposure.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 48 static findings across 5 files. All 22 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives: the asterisk character is used for NOS star ratings and markdown formatting, not cryptography. All 22 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives: these are markdown code blocks in documentation. All 8 'System reconnaissance' and 2 'Hardcoded URL' findings are false positives: they reference legitimate audit text and a GitHub source URL. The only true positive is a Python file write operation in extract_pdf.py that saves extracted PDF text locally, which is the documented purpose of the script. No malicious intent or data exfiltration patterns found.

content.user_title: Assess Case-Control Study Quality with the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale
content.value_statement: Medical researchers need to evaluate case-control study quality for systematic reviews and meta-analyses. This skill applies the validated Newcastle-Ottawa Scale to assess selection, comparability, and exposure domains with structured, reproducible output.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates case definition adequacy using primary record sources
Assesses case representativeness and control selection methods
Evaluates age and factor comparability between study groups
Reviews exposure ascertainment methods and non-response rates
Generates structured JSON and markdown table output with star ratings
Extracts text from PDF files for analysis input
limitations: Requires the full text of the case-control study to perform an accurate assessment
Cannot access paywalled or restricted journal articles directly
Does not replace human expert judgment for nuanced methodological decisions
The NOS star rating system may not capture all dimensions of study quality

use_cases.title: Systematic review quality screening
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a systematic review of case-control studies uses this skill to apply consistent NOS criteria across all included papers.

use_cases.title: Medical paper peer review
use_cases.description: A peer reviewer evaluates the methodological quality of a submitted case-control study manuscript using the structured NOS framework.

use_cases.title: Research methodology training
use_cases.description: A graduate student learns to apply the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale by practicing with sample studies and receiving structured feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Basic NOS quality assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate the following case-control study using the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale. Assess the Selection, Comparability, and Exposure domains. Study details: [insert study description or upload PDF].
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a single study assessment

prompt_templates.title: Batch study evaluation for systematic review
prompt_templates.prompt: I have [N] case-control studies for a systematic review. Evaluate each one using the NOS criteria. For each study, provide a structured JSON output with star ratings for Selection, Comparability, and Exposure. Then generate a summary comparison table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating multiple studies for a review project

prompt_templates.title: Detailed domain analysis with evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: For this case-control study, evaluate each NOS sub-criterion in detail. For each item, state the rating (star or no star), quote the relevant text from the study, and explain your reasoning in Chinese as required by the workflow.

prompt_templates.title: PDF-based assessment workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text from this PDF file: [path/to/study.pdf]. Then apply the full NOS assessment workflow: evaluate Selection, Comparability, and Exposure domains. Generate the final summary table using the formatting script. Study PDF: [insert file path].
prompt_templates.scenario: Assessing a study from a PDF document

output_examples.input: Evaluate a case-control study on the relationship between smoking and lung cancer using NOS criteria.
output: **Study:** Smoking and Lung Cancer Case-Control Study
- Definition of controls: No history of outcome (*)
- Other controlled factors: Smoking history adjusted (*)
- Same method for cases and controls: Yes (*)
- Non-response rate: Same rate for both groups (*)

output_examples.input: Compare the quality of three case-control studies on statin use and diabetes risk.
output: | Study | S1 | S2 | S3 | S4 | C1 | C2 | E1 | E2 | E3 | Total |
**Smith 2020:** Highest quality study with full marks across all domains.
**Jones 2021:** Limited by control representativeness and non-response rate reporting.
**Lee 2022:** Strong quality with minor limitation in control definition.

best_practices: Confirm the study design is a true case-control study before applying the NOS criteria
Quote specific text from the study to support each star rating decision
Document assumptions clearly when study details are incomplete or ambiguous
anti_patterns: Do not assign star ratings based on general impressions instead of specific NOS criteria
Do not skip the evidence quotation step when evaluating each domain item
Do not apply NOS criteria to study designs other than case-control studies

faq.question: What is the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale (NOS)?
faq.answer: The NOS is a validated tool for assessing the quality of non-randomized studies in meta-analyses. It evaluates case-control and cohort studies across three domains: Selection, Comparability, and Exposure, with a maximum of 9 stars.

faq.question: What inputs does this skill need to work?
faq.answer: The skill needs the full text or a PDF of a case-control study. You can provide the text directly or upload a PDF file for text extraction. The skill then evaluates the study against NOS criteria.

faq.question: Can this skill access paywalled journal articles?
faq.answer: No. This skill can only evaluate content that you provide directly. It cannot bypass paywalls or access restricted databases. You must supply the full study text.

faq.question: What does the output format look like?
faq.answer: The output includes a structured JSON assessment with star ratings for each NOS sub-criterion, plus a formatted markdown summary table showing the overall quality score.

faq.question: Can I evaluate multiple studies at once?
faq.answer: Yes. You can ask the skill to process multiple studies in a batch. It will generate individual assessments for each study and a comparison table summarizing all results.

faq.question: Does the NOS assessment replace expert review?
faq.answer: No. The NOS provides a structured framework for quality assessment, but it does not replace the informed judgment of subject matter experts. Use the results as a guide alongside expert evaluation.
